docs.title: Railway Provider
docs.text: This provider is used to interact with the many resources supported by Railway.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
This provider requires a Railway API token in order to manage resources.

To manage the full selection of resources, provide an account token from an account with appropriate permissions. Project tokens have limited permissions and cannot perform all operations — see the Authentication Guide for details on token types and their capabilities.

There are several ways to provide the required token:

  • Set the RAILWAY_TOKEN environment variable (recommended). The provider can read the RAILWAY_TOKEN environment variable and the token stored there to authenticate.
  • Set the token argument in the provider configuration. You can set the token argument in the provider configuration. Use an input variable for the token.

docs.title: Guides
docs.text: • Authentication Guide — Token types, permissions, and troubleshooting.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text: provider "railway" {}

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• api_url (String) Override the Railway API URL. Used for testing.
  • token (String, Sensitive) The token used to authenticate with Railway.

docs.title: Railway OpenTofu Provider
docs.text:
An OpenTofu provider for managing Railway infrastructure as code. Also compatible with Terraform.

17 resources, 3 data sources, full import support.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Add the provider to your required_providers block:

terraform {
  required_providers {
    railway = {
      source  = "jamesprnich/railway"
      version = "~> 0.8.0"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Set the RAILWAY_TOKEN environment variable:

export RAILWAY_TOKEN="your-token"

Or configure it in the provider block:

provider "railway" {
  token = var.railway_token
}

Token types

Railway has two token types with very different permissions. Account tokens are recommended — they have full access to all resources. Project tokens are scoped to a single environment and cannot attach sources to services. See the Authentication Guide for details.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
resource "railway_project" "main" {
  name = "my-app"
}

resource "railway_service" "postgres" {
  name         = "postgres"
  project_id   = railway_project.main.id
  source_image = "postgres:17.5-alpine"

  volume = {
    name       = "pgdata"
    mount_path = "/var/lib/postgresql/data"
  }
}

resource "railway_variable" "postgres_password" {
  name           = "POSTGRES_PASSWORD"
  value          = var.postgres_password
  environment_id = railway_project.main.default_environment.id
  service_id     = railway_service.postgres.id
}

tofu apply -var='postgres_password=secretpassword'

docs.title: Resources
docs.text: Resource Purpose railway_project Project with default environment railway_environment Additional environment within a project railway_service Service (Docker image or GitHub repo) with optional inline volume railway_service_instance Per-environment config: source, build, deploy settings, resource limits railway_variable Environment variable scoped to service + environment railway_variable_collection Bulk environment variables for a service + environment railway_shared_variable Project-wide variable (not scoped to a service) railway_volume Standalone persistent volume railway_volume_backup_schedule Automatic backup schedule for a volume instance railway_service_domain Auto-generated .up.railway.app domain railway_custom_domain Custom domain with DNS verification railway_tcp_proxy TCP proxy for non-HTTP services railway_deployment_trigger Auto-deploy from GitHub/GitLab on push railway_egress_gateway Static egress IP for external service allowlisting railway_private_network Private network for internal service-to-service communication railway_private_network_endpoint Connects a service to a private network with DNS name railway_project_token Project-scoped deploy token (sensitive) for CI/CD railway_trusted_domain Workspace-level trusted domain for SSO railway_notification_rule Notification rule (webhooks, Slack, email) — replaces railway_webhook railway_bucket S3-compatible object storage bucket railway_ssh_public_key SSH public key for workspace railway_project_member Project membership with role

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data Source Lookup By data.railway_project id or name data.railway_environment id or project_id + name data.railway_service id or project_id + name

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• Private networking requires a manual redeploy of the target service after the first provider-created deployment (Railway platform bug with Wireguard tunnel setup).
  • Service domain subdomains are auto-generated and cannot be customized via the API. Use railway_custom_domain for specific domain names.
  • vcpus and memory_gb on railway_service_instance are write-only — they can be set but not read back. Import will not capture them.
  • Multiple redeployments on first apply — Railway triggers a redeployment on every API mutation (source connection, variables, instance config). First tofu apply creates 3-4 deployments per service. Use railway_variable_collection instead of individual variables to minimise this. Subsequent applies with no changes trigger zero redeployments.

docs.title: AI Agent Integration
docs.text: This provider ships with SKILL.md — a structured reference designed for AI coding agents. It contains every resource, every field, every import format, working examples, and known issues. Point your AI agent at this file and it can deploy full Railway infrastructure autonomously.

docs.title: railway_environment (Data Source)
docs.text: Look up an existing Railway environment by ID or by name within a project.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
# Look up by name (requires project_id)
data "railway_environment" "staging" {
  name       = "staging"
  project_id = railway_project.example.id
}

# Or look up by ID
data "railway_environment" "by_id" {
  id = "your-environment-id"
}

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the environment. Exactly one of id or name must be provided.
  • name (String) Name of the environment. Exactly one of id or name must be provided. Requires project_id.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the environment belongs to. Required when looking up by name.

docs.title: railway_project (Data Source)
docs.text: Look up an existing Railway project by ID or name.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
# Look up by name
data "railway_project" "by_name" {
  name = "my-project"
}

# Or look up by ID
data "railway_project" "by_id" {
  id = "your-project-id"
}

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the project. Exactly one of id or name must be provided.
  • name (String) Name of the project. Exactly one of id or name must be provided.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • description (String) Description of the project.

docs.title: railway_service (Data Source)
docs.text: Look up an existing Railway service by ID or by name within a project.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
# Look up by name (requires project_id)
data "railway_service" "api" {
  name       = "api"
  project_id = railway_project.example.id
}

# Or look up by ID
data "railway_service" "by_id" {
  id = "your-service-id"
}

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the service. Exactly one of id or name must be provided.
  • name (String) Name of the service. Exactly one of id or name must be provided. Requires project_id.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the service belongs to. Required when looking up by name. Computed when looking up by id.

docs.title: Authentication Guide
docs.text: The Railway provider supports two types of API tokens. Choosing the right token type is critical for both security and functionality — they have very different permission levels.

docs.title: Account Tokens (Recommended)
docs.text:
Account tokens have full access to all projects and resources within your Railway account.

How to create: Railway Dashboard > Account Settings > Tokens > Create Token

Permissions: - Create, read, update, and delete services - Connect Docker images and GitHub repos to services (serviceConnect) - Manage volumes, domains, variables, and all other resources - Access all projects in the account

When to use: Any workflow that needs to create or fully configure services. This is the token type you need for most provider operations.

Security note: Account tokens are powerful. Never commit them to source control. Use environment variables or a secrets manager:

export RAILWAY_TOKEN="your-account-token"

docs.title: Project Tokens
docs.text:
Project tokens are scoped to a single environment within a single project. They provide strong isolation but have limited permissions.

How to create: Railway Dashboard > Project > Settings > Tokens > Create Token

Permissions: - Read project, environment, and service metadata - Create and delete services - Manage deployment triggers, egress gateways, private networks - Delete resources within the scoped environment

Limitations (will cause errors): - Cannot run serviceConnect — this means you cannot attach a Docker image or GitHub repo to a service. Any railway_service resource with source_image or source_repo set will partially create (the service is created but the source is not attached) and then error. - Cannot list all projects — the data.railway_project data source with a name lookup will fail. Direct id lookup works. - Cannot query across environments — only the environment the token is scoped to is accessible.

Authentication header: Project tokens use a different HTTP header than account tokens. The provider handles this automatically by sending both headers: - Authorization: Bearer <token> (used by account tokens) - Project-Access-Token: <token> (used by project tokens)

When to use: Read-only operations, managing resources on existing services, or CI/CD pipelines that only need to trigger deployments within a specific environment.

docs.title: Token Type Comparison
docs.text: Capability Account Token Project Token Create services Yes Yes Attach Docker image / repo (serviceConnect) Yes No Create volumes Yes Yes Manage domains Yes Yes Manage deployment triggers Yes Yes Manage egress gateways Yes Yes Manage private networks Yes Yes Manage variables Yes Yes List all projects Yes No Access multiple environments Yes No Access multiple projects Yes No

docs.title: Recommended Setup
docs.text:
For a typical workflow managing Railway infrastructure:

  1. Create a dedicated account token for automation
  2. Store it in your CI/CD secrets or a secrets manager
  3. Scope your configs to specific projects using project_id — the isolation comes from your config, not the token
  4. Rotate tokens periodically via the Railway dashboard
provider "railway" {
  # Set via RAILWAY_TOKEN environment variable
  # Do not hardcode tokens in config files
}

resource "railway_service" "web" {
  name         = "my-app"
  project_id   = "your-project-id"
  source_image = "nginx:1.27.5-alpine"
}

docs.title: "Not Authorized" errors
docs.text:
If you see Not Authorized errors, check:

  1. Token type — Are you using a project token for an operation that requires an account token? See the comparison table above.
  2. Environment scope — Project tokens only work within their scoped environment. Passing a different environment_id will fail.
  3. Token validity — Tokens can be revoked from the Railway dashboard. Generate a new one if in doubt.

docs.title: "serviceConnect Not Authorized"
docs.text: This specifically means you're using a project token and trying to set source_image or source_repo on a service. Switch to an account token.

docs.title: railway_bucket (Resource)
docs.text:
Railway bucket. S3-compatible object storage bucket attached to a project.

~> Note: Railway does not currently expose a delete mutation for buckets. Running tofu destroy will remove the bucket from Terraform state but the bucket itself will remain in Railway. To fully remove a bucket, delete the entire project or remove the bucket via the Railway dashboard. Once Railway adds a delete API, this provider will be updated.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_bucket" "data" {
  name       = "user-uploads"
  project_id = railway_project.main.id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• name (String) Name of the bucket.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the bucket belongs to.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the bucket.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:bucket_id
tofu import railway_bucket.data your-project-id:your-bucket-id

docs.title: railway_custom_domain (Resource)
docs.text: Railway custom domain.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_custom_domain" "api" {
  domain         = "api.example.com"
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_id     = railway_service.api.id

  # Optional
  # target_port = 8080
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• domain (String) Custom domain.
  • environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the custom domain belongs to.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the custom domain belongs to.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: • target_port (Number) Target port for the custom domain.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• dns_record_value (String) DNS record value of the custom domain.
  • host_label (String) Host label of the custom domain.
  • id (String) Identifier of the custom domain.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the custom domain belongs to.
  • zone (String) Zone of the custom domain.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_name:domain
tofu import railway_custom_domain.api your-service-id:production:api.example.com

docs.title: railway_deployment_trigger (Resource)
docs.text: Railway deployment trigger. Connects a source code repository to a service so that pushes to a branch automatically trigger deployments.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_deployment_trigger" "api" {
  project_id      = railway_project.example.id
  environment_id  = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_id      = railway_service.api.id
  repository      = "myorg/myapp"
  branch          = "main"
  source_provider = "github"

  # Optional
  # check_suites   = true
  # root_directory = "/backend"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• branch (String) Branch to watch for changes.
  • environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the deployment trigger belongs to.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the deployment trigger belongs to.
  • repository (String) Repository to watch for changes (e.g. "owner/repo").
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the deployment trigger belongs to.
  • source_provider (String) Source provider (e.g. "github").

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• check_suites (Boolean) Whether to wait for check suites to pass before deploying.
  • root_directory (String) Root directory within the repository. Only relevant for monorepos.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the deployment trigger.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:environment_id:service_id:trigger_id
tofu import railway_deployment_trigger.api your-project-id:your-environment-id:your-service-id:your-trigger-id

docs.title: railway_egress_gateway (Resource)
docs.text: Railway egress gateway. Associates a static egress IP with a service in a specific environment.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_egress_gateway" "api" {
  service_id     = railway_service.api.id
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id

  # Optional
  # region = "us-west1"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the egress gateway belongs to.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the egress gateway belongs to.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: • region (String) Region for the egress gateway.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the egress gateway (service_id:environment_id).
  • ip_addresses (List of String) List of static IPv4 addresses assigned to the egress gateway.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_id
tofu import railway_egress_gateway.api your-service-id:your-environment-id

docs.title: railway_environment (Resource)
docs.text: Railway environment.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_environment" "staging" {
  name       = "staging"
  project_id = railway_project.example.id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• name (String) Name of the environment.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the environment belongs to.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the environment.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:environment_name
tofu import railway_environment.staging your-project-id:staging

docs.title: railway_notification_rule (Resource)
docs.text: Railway notification rule. Sends notifications (via webhook, Slack, email, or other channels) when events of the configured types occur. Replaces the deprecated railway_webhook resource — webhooks are now one channel type among many.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_notification_rule" "deploy_alerts" {
  workspace_id = var.railway_workspace_id
  project_id   = railway_project.main.id
  event_types  = ["deployment.completed", "deployment.failed"]
  severities   = ["CRITICAL", "WARNING"]
  channel_configs = [
    jsonencode({
      type = "webhook"
      url  = "https://example.com/railway-hook"
    }),
  ]
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• channel_configs (List of String) List of channel configurations as JSON strings. Each entry describes one delivery channel (webhook, Slack, email, etc.). Refer to the Railway API documentation for the supported channel shapes. Example: jsonencode({type = "webhook", url = "https://example.com/hook"}).
  • event_types (List of String) Event types that trigger the rule (e.g. deployment.completed, deployment.failed).
  • workspace_id (String) Identifier of the workspace the rule belongs to.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• ephemeral_environments (Boolean) Whether to notify for events on ephemeral (PR) environments.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the rule scopes to. When omitted, the rule applies to the entire workspace.
  • severities (List of String) Severity levels to notify on. Each value must be one of CRITICAL, INFO, NOTICE, WARNING.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the notification rule.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by workspace_id:rule_id
tofu import railway_notification_rule.deploy_alerts your-workspace-id:your-rule-id

docs.title: railway_private_network (Resource)
docs.text: Railway private network. Creates a private network in a specific environment for internal service-to-service communication.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_private_network" "internal" {
  project_id     = railway_project.example.id
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  name           = "internal"

  # Optional
  # tags = ["backend"]
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the private network belongs to.
  • name (String) Name of the private network.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• project_id (String) Identifier of the project the private network belongs to. Required for creation, populated automatically on import.
  • tags (List of String) Tags associated with the private network.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• dns_name (String) DNS name of the private network.
  • id (String) Identifier of the private network (publicId).
  • network_id (Number) Numeric network identifier.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by environment_id:network_public_id
tofu import railway_private_network.internal your-environment-id:your-network-public-id

docs.title: railway_private_network_endpoint (Resource)
docs.text: Railway private network endpoint. Connects a service to a private network, enabling internal service-to-service communication.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_private_network_endpoint" "api" {
  private_network_id = railway_private_network.internal.id
  service_id         = railway_service.api.id
  environment_id     = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_name       = railway_service.api.name

  # Optional
  # dns_name = "api-internal"
  # tags     = ["backend"]
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment.
  • private_network_id (String) Identifier of the private network to connect to.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service to connect.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• dns_name (String) DNS name of the endpoint within the private network. Can be changed via rename.
  • service_name (String) Name of the service. Required for creation (used in the create API input), but not needed for import since it is not returned by the API.
  • tags (List of String) Tags associated with the private network endpoint.
  • timeouts (Attributes) (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the private network endpoint (publicId).
  • private_ips (List of String) List of private IP addresses assigned to the endpoint.

docs.title: Nested Schema for timeouts
docs.text:
Optional:

  • create (String) A string that can be parsed as a duration consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
  • read (String) A string that can be parsed as a duration consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by environment_id:private_network_id:service_id
tofu import railway_private_network_endpoint.api your-environment-id:your-private-network-id:your-service-id

docs.title: railway_project (Resource)
docs.text: Railway project.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_project" "example" {
  name = "my-project"

  # Optional
  # description    = "My Railway project"
  # private        = true
  # has_pr_deploys = false
  # default_environment = {
  #   name = "production"
  # }
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text: • name (String) Name of the project.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• default_environment (Attributes) Default environment of the project. When multiple exist, the oldest is considered. (see below for nested schema)
  • description (String) Description of the project.
  • has_pr_deploys (Boolean) Whether the project has PR deploys enabled. Default false.
  • private (Boolean) Privacy of the project. Default true.
  • workspace_id (String) Identifier of the workspace the project belongs to. Required if the railway token has access to multiple workspaces. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the project.

docs.title: Nested Schema for default_environment
docs.text:
Optional:

  • name (String) Name of the default environment.

Read-Only:

  • id (String) Identifier of the default environment.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project ID
tofu import railway_project.example your-project-id

docs.title: railway_project_member (Resource)
docs.text: Railway project membership. Adds a user to a project with a given role.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_project_member" "alice" {
  project_id = railway_project.main.id
  user_id    = "user-12345"
  role       = "MEMBER"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• project_id (String) Identifier of the project.
  • role (String) Role assigned to the member. One of ADMIN, MEMBER, VIEWER.
  • user_id (String) Identifier of the user to add as a member.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• email (String) Email address of the member.
  • id (String) Identifier of the project membership.
  • name (String) Display name of the member.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:user_id
tofu import railway_project_member.alice your-project-id:your-user-id

docs.title: railway_project_token (Resource)
docs.text:
Railway project token. Generates a scoped deploy token for CI/CD pipelines.

~> Note: The token attribute is only available at creation time. Railway does not return the raw token on subsequent reads. Store it in your CI secret manager immediately after tofu apply.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_project_token" "ci" {
  name           = "github-actions"
  project_id     = railway_project.example.id
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
}

output "deploy_token" {
  value     = railway_project_token.ci.token
  sensitive = true
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the token has access to.
  • name (String) Name of the project token.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the token belongs to.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the project token.
  • token (String, Sensitive) The raw token value. Only populated at creation; null on import or read.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:token_id.
# The raw token value is NOT recoverable on import — regenerate the token if you need it.
tofu import railway_project_token.ci your-project-id:your-token-id

docs.title: railway_service (Resource)
docs.text:
Railway service.

⚠️ NOTE: All the other settings not specified here are recommended to be specified in the Railway config file.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_service" "api" {
  name       = "api"
  project_id = railway_project.example.id

  # Optional: deploy from a Docker image
  # source_image = "nginx:1.27-alpine"

  # Optional: deploy from a GitHub repo (requires both)
  # source_repo        = "myorg/myapp"
  # source_repo_branch = "main"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• name (String) Name of the service.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the service belongs to. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• config_path (String) Path to the Railway config file. Conflicts with source_image.
  • cron_schedule (String) Cron schedule of the service. Only allowed when total number of replicas across all regions is 1.
  • regions (Attributes List) Regions with replicas to deploy service in. (see below for nested schema)
  • root_directory (String) Directory to use for the service. Conflicts with source_image.
  • source_image (String) Source image of the service. Conflicts with source_repo, source_repo_branch, root_directory and config_path.
  • source_image_registry_password (String, Sensitive) Private Docker registry credentials.
  • source_image_registry_username (String) Private Docker registry credentials.
  • source_repo (String) Source repository of the service. Conflicts with source_image.
  • source_repo_branch (String) Source repository branch to be used with source_repo. Must be specified if source_repo is specified.
  • volume (Attributes) Volume connected to the service. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the service.

docs.title: Nested Schema for regions
docs.text:
Optional:

  • num_replicas (Number) Number of replicas to deploy. Default 1.
  • region (String) Region to deploy in.

docs.title: Nested Schema for volume
docs.text:
Required:

  • mount_path (String) Mount path of the volume.
  • name (String) Name of the volume.

Read-Only:

  • id (String) Identifier of the volume.
  • size (Number) Size of the volume in MB.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service ID
tofu import railway_service.api your-service-id

docs.title: railway_service_domain (Resource)
docs.text: Railway service domain. Creates a Railway-provided .up.railway.app domain for a service. The subdomain is auto-generated by Railway and cannot be customized. For custom subdomains, use railway_custom_domain instead.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_service_domain" "api" {
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_id     = railway_service.api.id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the service domain belongs to.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the service domain belongs to.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• domain (String) Full domain name (e.g. my-service-abc1.up.railway.app).
  • id (String) Identifier of the service domain.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the service domain belongs to.
  • subdomain (String) Subdomain of the service domain. Auto-generated by Railway.
  • suffix (String) Suffix of the service domain (e.g. up.railway.app).

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_name:full_domain
tofu import railway_service_domain.api your-service-id:production:my-app-abc123.up.railway.app

docs.title: railway_service_instance (Resource)
docs.text: Railway service instance. Configures a service in a specific environment, including source, build, deploy settings, and resource limits.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_service_instance" "api_staging" {
  service_id     = railway_service.api.id
  environment_id = railway_environment.staging.id

  # Optional: override source for this environment (public image)
  # source_image = "myorg/myapp:staging"

  # Optional: private registry credentials (required when source_image is a private image)
  # registry_credentials = {
  #   username = "myuser"
  #   password = var.registry_token   # mark as sensitive in your vars
  # }

  # Optional: build and deploy settings
  # build_command    = "npm run build"
  # start_command    = "npm start"
  # healthcheck_path = "/health"
  # num_replicas     = 1
  # region           = "us-west1"
  # vcpus            = 2
  # memory_gb        = 0.5

  # Optional: deploy behavior
  # sleep_application          = true
  # overlap_seconds            = 5
  # draining_seconds           = 10
  # healthcheck_timeout        = 300
  # restart_policy_type        = "ON_FAILURE"
  # restart_policy_max_retries = 3
  # builder                    = "RAILPACK"
  # watch_patterns             = ["backend/**"]
  # pre_deploy_command         = ["python manage.py migrate"]
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• build_command (String) Custom build command.
  • builder (String) Builder to use. Valid values: HEROKU, NIXPACKS, PAKETO, RAILPACK.
  • config_path (String) Path to the Railway config file (e.g. backend/railway.toml).
  • cron_schedule (String) Cron schedule for the service. Can only be set when num_replicas is 1.
  • draining_seconds (Number) Number of seconds to drain connections before stopping the old deployment.
  • healthcheck_path (String) HTTP path for health checks.
  • healthcheck_timeout (Number) Healthcheck timeout in seconds.
  • memory_gb (Number) Amount of memory in GB to allocate (e.g. 0.5, 1, 2). Maps to Railway's container memory limit.
  • num_replicas (Number) Number of replicas.
  • overlap_seconds (Number) Number of seconds to keep the old deployment running alongside the new one during a rollout.
  • pre_deploy_command (List of String) Pre-deploy command(s) to run before starting the service.
  • region (String) Region to deploy the service instance in.
  • registry_credentials (Attributes) Credentials for a private Docker registry. Required when source_image references a private image. Only available on Railway Pro plan. The password is write-only — it is sent to Railway on create/update but is never returned on read; plan output will not show diffs on the password after the initial apply. Important: always source password from a sensitive Terraform variable or a secrets data source (e.g. Vault). Ensure your Terraform state backend uses encryption at rest — the credential is stored in state for the lifetime of the resource. (see below for nested schema)
  • restart_policy_max_retries (Number) Maximum number of restart retries when restart_policy_type is ON_FAILURE.
  • restart_policy_type (String) Restart policy type. Valid values: ALWAYS, ON_FAILURE, NEVER.
  • root_directory (String) Root directory for the service within the repository.
  • sleep_application (Boolean) Whether the service should sleep when inactive.
  • source_image (String) Docker image to use as the source (e.g. postgres:17). Conflicts with source_repo.
  • source_repo (String) GitHub repository to use as the source (e.g. owner/repo). Conflicts with source_image.
  • start_command (String) Custom start command.
  • vcpus (Number) Number of vCPUs to allocate (e.g. 0.5, 1, 2). Maps to Railway's container CPU limit.
  • watch_patterns (List of String) File patterns to watch for changes to trigger rebuilds (e.g. ["server/**"]).

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the service instance (service_id:environment_id).

docs.title: Nested Schema for registry_credentials
docs.text:
Required:

  • password (String, Sensitive) Registry password or access token.
  • username (String) Registry username.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_id
tofu import railway_service_instance.api_staging your-service-id:your-environment-id

docs.title: railway_shared_variable (Resource)
docs.text: Railway shared variable.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_shared_variable" "sentry_dsn" {
  name           = "SENTRY_DSN"
  value          = "https://key@sentry.io/123"
  project_id     = railway_project.example.id
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the variable belongs to.
  • name (String) Name of the variable.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the variable belongs to.
  • value (String, Sensitive) Value of the variable.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the variable.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:environment_name:variable_name
tofu import railway_shared_variable.sentry_dsn your-project-id:production:SENTRY_DSN

docs.title: railway_ssh_public_key (Resource)
docs.text: Railway SSH public key. Registers an SSH key against a workspace (or the authenticated user if workspace_id is omitted) for use with Railway features that require SSH authentication.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_ssh_public_key" "ci" {
  name         = "github-actions"
  public_key   = file("~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub")
  workspace_id = var.railway_workspace_id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• name (String) Friendly name for the SSH key.
  • public_key (String) OpenSSH-format public key (e.g. ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host).

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: • workspace_id (String) Identifier of the workspace the key belongs to. If omitted, the key is registered against the authenticated user.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• fingerprint (String) Server-computed fingerprint of the public key.
  • id (String) Identifier of the SSH key.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by key_id
tofu import railway_ssh_public_key.ci your-key-id

docs.title: railway_tcp_proxy (Resource)
docs.text: Railway TCP proxy.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_tcp_proxy" "redis" {
  application_port = 6379
  environment_id   = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_id       = railway_service.redis.id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• application_port (Number) Port of the application the TCP proxy points to.
  • environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the TCP proxy belongs to.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the TCP proxy belongs to.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• domain (String) Domain of the TCP proxy.
  • id (String) Identifier of the TCP proxy.
  • proxy_port (Number) Port of the TCP proxy.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_id:tcp_proxy_id
tofu import railway_tcp_proxy.redis your-service-id:your-environment-id:your-tcp-proxy-id

docs.title: railway_trusted_domain (Resource)
docs.text: Railway workspace trusted domain. Used for SSO and access control. The domain must be verified via DNS before it is honoured.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_trusted_domain" "company" {
  workspace_id = var.railway_workspace_id
  domain_name  = "example.com"
  role         = "MEMBER"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• domain_name (String) The fully-qualified domain name to trust (e.g. example.com).
  • role (String) Role assigned to users authenticating from this domain (e.g. MEMBER).
  • workspace_id (String) Identifier of the workspace the trusted domain belongs to.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the trusted domain.
  • status (String) Verification status of the trusted domain. One of PENDING, VERIFIED, FAILED.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by workspace_id:trusted_domain_id
tofu import railway_trusted_domain.company your-workspace-id:your-trusted-domain-id

docs.title: railway_variable (Resource)
docs.text: Railway variable. Any changes in collection triggers service redeployment.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_variable" "database_url" {
  name           = "DATABASE_URL"
  value          = "postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db"
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_id     = railway_service.api.id
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the variable belongs to.
  • name (String) Name of the variable.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the variable belongs to.
  • value (String, Sensitive) Value of the variable.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the variable.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the variable belongs to.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_name:variable_name
tofu import railway_variable.database_url your-service-id:production:DATABASE_URL

docs.title: railway_variable_collection (Resource)
docs.text: Railway variable collection. Group of variables managed as a whole. Any changes in collection triggers service redeployment.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_variable_collection" "app_config" {
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  service_id     = railway_service.api.id

  variables = [
    {
      name  = "DATABASE_URL"
      value = "postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db"
    },
    {
      name  = "REDIS_URL"
      value = "redis://host:6379"
    },
  ]
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the variable collection belongs to.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the variable collection belongs to.
  • variables (Attributes List) Collection of variables. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the variable collection.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the variable collection belongs to.

docs.title: Nested Schema for variables
docs.text:
Required:

  • name (String) Name of the variable.
  • value (String, Sensitive) Value of the variable.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by service_id:environment_name:var_name1:var_name2:...
tofu import railway_variable_collection.app_config your-service-id:production:DATABASE_URL:REDIS_URL

docs.title: railway_volume (Resource)
docs.text: Railway volume. Creates a persistent volume attached to a service in a specific environment.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_volume" "data" {
  project_id     = railway_project.example.id
  service_id     = railway_service.postgres.id
  environment_id = railway_project.example.default_environment.id
  mount_path     = "/var/lib/postgresql/data"

  # Optional
  # name = "postgres-data"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• environment_id (String) Identifier of the environment the volume belongs to. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.
  • mount_path (String) Mount path of the volume in the container.
  • project_id (String) Identifier of the project the volume belongs to. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.
  • service_id (String) Identifier of the service the volume is attached to. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: • name (String) Name of the volume.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Identifier of the volume.
  • size_mb (Number) Size of the volume in MB.
  • volume_instance_id (String) Identifier of the volume instance. Use this to reference the volume in railway_volume_backup_schedule.

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by project_id:volume_id:service_id:environment_id
tofu import railway_volume.data your-project-id:your-volume-id:your-service-id:your-environment-id

docs.title: railway_volume_backup_schedule (Resource)
docs.text: Railway volume backup schedule. Manages the backup schedule for a volume instance.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "railway_volume_backup_schedule" "daily" {
  volume_instance_id = railway_volume.data.volume_instance_id
  kinds              = ["DAILY"]

  # Valid values: "DAILY", "WEEKLY", "MONTHLY"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• kinds (List of String) List of backup schedule kinds to enable. Valid values: DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY.
  • volume_instance_id (String) Identifier of the volume instance. ~> Warning: Changing this forces resource destruction and recreation.

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text: • id (String) Identifier of the volume backup schedule (same as volume_instance_id).

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
Import is supported using the following syntax:

# Import by volume_instance_id
tofu import railway_volume_backup_schedule.daily your-volume-instance-id
